---
title: "Catastrophe ELT Primer Quickstart"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: true
    template: ../doc_template.html
    embed-resources: true
---

## Purpose

This companion gives the shortest path to build and render the internal Release 1 package.

Release 1 package components:

1. Canonical primer: `elt_primer.qmd`
2. Quickstart companion: `primer_quickstart.qmd`
3. Executable index: `executable_index.qmd`

## Fast Reading Routes Into The Primer

If you want to read first and run later, use one of these quick routes in `elt_primer.qmd`:

1. 10-minute route:
  Audience and Intent -> What "ELT" Means in Practice -> OEP and AEP: Choosing the Right Lens -> Release Readiness Checklist
2. 30-minute route:
  ELT Structure, Variants, and Loss Views -> From ELT to Empirical Distributions -> End-to-end mini-case: synthetic ELT to treaty outputs -> Assumptions Register
3. Deep technical route:
  Full core sections -> Advanced Methods (After the Practical Core) -> Production Implementation and Governance

## High-Value Sections Added Recently

These are useful entry points if you already know the basics:

1. Generating Synthetic ELTs (Why and How)
2. From ELT to Empirical Distributions (expanded step-by-step conversion)
3. End-to-end mini-case: synthetic ELT to treaty outputs
4. Decision communication template (pricing committee ready)
5. Glossary (Quick Reference)

## Build Container

```bash
just image-build
```

## Check Available Targets

```bash
just --list
```

## Render (Dev)

```bash
just render-dev-container
```

## Render (Full)

```bash
just render-full-container
```

## Outputs

Rendered files are written to `outputs/`.

- `elt_primer.html`
- `primer_quickstart.html`
- `executable_index.html`

## Determinism

Configuration is in `render.env`.

- `DEV_N_SIMS`
- `FULL_N_SIMS`
- `SEED_R`
- `SEED_PY`

## Recommended Working Loop

1. Edit content in `elt_primer.qmd`.
2. Run `just render-dev-container` for fast feedback.
3. Run `just render-full-container` before sharing or review.
4. Check `outputs/` artifacts for consistency.
